questions.text: The angle of elevation is measured from:
questions.explanation: Angle of elevation is the angle formed by the line of sight with the horizontal when looking upward.

questions.text: Angle of elevation equals angle of depression.
questions.explanation: When two observers look at each other, the angle of elevation for one equals the angle of depression for the other (alternate angles).

questions.correctAnswer: The angle formed by line of sight with horizontal when looking downward.
questions.explanation: Angle of depression is measured from the horizontal downward to the line of sight when looking at an object below.

questions.text: Trigonometry is used to find heights and ________.
questions.explanation: Trigonometry is used to find heights of buildings, towers, and distances of ships from shore, etc.
